skill.name: writing-shape
skill.description: Turns raw Markdown notes into a publishable article through a guided writing session. It drafts openings, builds the article paragraph by paragraph, and helps choose the right format for each section.
skill.summary: Shape raw Markdown notes into a polished article through a structured writing session.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported shell backticks, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Review found these are false positives from prose and Markdown formatting examples, with no shell execution, cryptography, network access, or prompt injection. The only meaningful concern is normal user-directed file reading and writing for the article workflow.

low_findings.title: User-Directed File Modification
low_findings.description: The skill reads a user-provided Markdown input and appends agreed text to a separate article file. This is expected behavior for the workflow, but it can modify files at paths chosen during the session.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file read and write behavior is explicit in the skill instructions. It is low risk because writes are user-directed and scoped to the article workflow.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md line 49 is inline Markdown showing callout syntax. No executable command or shell invocation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks wrap literal Markdown callout examples, not a command string. There is no surrounding code context that could execute them.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptographic algorithm matches at SKILL.md lines 3, 24, and 49 are prose or Markdown examples. No cryptographic function, hash algorithm, or security-sensitive transformation appears in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only writing instructions at these lines. There is no code path involving MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithms.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance pattern at SKILL.md line 12 asks for an output path and says to re-read the article before writing. This preserves user edits and does not enumerate system information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The instruction concerns a user-selected article path and edit preservation. No host inventory, credential discovery, environment probing, or directory scanning is requested.

content.value_statement: Raw notes often contain useful ideas but lack a clear argument and order. This skill turns Markdown source material into a structured article through guided drafting and revision.
actual_capabilities: Reads a Markdown file of notes or rough material before drafting.
Creates two or three candidate openings with different article angles.
Builds the article paragraph by paragraph through conversation.
Compares prose, lists, tables, callouts, quotes, and code blocks.
Appends agreed sections to a separate article file during the session.
Re-reads the article file before each write to preserve user edits.
limitations: Requires a Markdown source file or equivalent raw material from the user.
Does not edit the original raw material file.
Does not publish the article or format it for a specific platform by default.
Does not invent missing examples without asking the user.

use_cases.title: Turn Research Notes Into a Draft
use_cases.description: Convert scattered Markdown notes into an article with a clear opening, argument, and sequence.

use_cases.title: Develop Technical Blog Posts
use_cases.description: Shape code notes, examples, and observations into a readable technical article.

use_cases.title: Refine Internal Knowledge
use_cases.description: Transform meeting notes or internal fragments into a coherent document for teammates.

prompt_templates.title: Start From Notes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use writing-shape on my Markdown notes at [path]. Help me choose an opening and build the article step by step.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Article Angles
prompt_templates.prompt: Read my source notes and draft three possible openings. Make each opening argue for a different direction.

prompt_templates.title: Build With Format Decisions
prompt_templates.scenario: Draft while choosing the right structure for each section.
prompt_templates.prompt: Shape this material into an article. For each next section, explain whether it should be prose, a list, a table, a quote, or a callout.

prompt_templates.title: Collaborative Article Revision
prompt_templates.scenario: Continue a live draft while preserving edits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Continue shaping the article at [article path] from the source file at [notes path]. Re-read the article before each edit and only change the section we discuss.

output_examples.input: A Markdown file with loose research notes and no clear introduction.
output_examples.output: Three distinct opening options, each with a different thesis and suggested article direction.

output_examples.input: A rough draft with mixed paragraphs, lists, and examples.
output_examples.output: A revised next section with a short explanation of why prose, a list, or a callout fits the reader's need.

output_examples.input: An article file that the user is editing during the session.
output_examples.output: Incremental additions that preserve existing user edits and keep the draft moving in order.

best_practices: Provide the raw Markdown path and the desired article path before drafting starts.
Choose an opening before asking for body sections to avoid a scattered article.
Review each proposed section before it is appended to the article file.
anti_patterns: Do not use it when you need a finished article without interactive decisions.
Do not expect it to invent missing research or examples without user input.
Do not point it at files that should not be read or modified.

faq.question: Does this skill write the whole article at once?
faq.answer: No. It works paragraph by paragraph so the user can steer the argument and structure.

faq.question: Will it change my source notes?
faq.answer: No. The skill treats the raw material file as read-only and writes to a separate article file.

faq.question: Can it use tables and callouts?
faq.answer: Yes. It discusses whether prose, lists, tables, callouts, quotes, or code blocks fit each section.

faq.question: Is it suitable for technical writing?
faq.answer: Yes. It can shape technical notes into articles, especially when the source material includes examples or code context.

faq.question: Does it publish the finished article?
faq.answer: No. Publishing and platform-specific formatting are outside the default scope.

faq.question: Can I edit the draft during the session?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill instructs the assistant to re-read the article file before each write to preserve user edits.
